catalysts.catalyst_details: Expected to be in-line to slightly better than consensus due to strong AI demand, stabilization in non-AI semi business, and strong VMware momentum.
catalysts.catalyst_dates: Apr quarter (F2Q25) results, reported around May 30, 2025
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Broadcom is set to report Apr quarter (F2Q25) earnings Thursday, and we continued to see a strong demand profile for its AI products (custom ASICs and networking solutions), stabilization in the non-AI semi business (e.g., enterprise, server/storage, broadband, wireless) and continued VMware revenue synergy unlock. As a result, we expect Apr-qtr revenue/earnings/FCF to be in-line to slightly better vs our/consensus expectations. • Expect in-line to slightly better Apr-Qtr results on continued strong AI demand, stabilization in non-AI semi business, and continued strong VMWare momentum. We believe that Apr quarter revenue/earnings/FCF were in-line to slightly better than ours/consensus expectations on AI strength and continued solid software renewals in its VMware business.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Expected to be up 5-7% Q/Q growth, in-line to slightly better than consensus, closer to J.P. Morgan's $16.1B estimate, led by AI demand strength.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Off of a higher Apr-rev Qtr base, we see Jul-Qtr revenue guidance up 5-7% Q/Q growth, in-line to slightly better than consensus expectation and closer to our above-Street estimates of $16.1B led by AI demand strength (we estimate AI revenues of $5B+ vs consensus of $4.8B). • Expect Jul-Qtr guide to be in-line to slightly better vs expectation on continued AI strength (custom ASICs and networking) and gradually improving trends in non-AI semi business….start of the ramp of its 3nm TPU ASIC programs... Off of a higher Apr-rev Qtr base, we see Jul-Qtr revenue guidance in-line to slightly better than consensus and closer to our above-Street estimates of $16.1B.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip production ramp
catalysts.catalyst_details: Start of the production ramp for Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip, expected to be a strong growth driver for Jul-Qtr and remainder of the year, and into FY26. Estimated to drive >$15B in lifetime revenues and be the most powerful custom XPU AI accelerator.
catalysts.catalyst_dates: Jul-Qtr and remainder of FY25, into FY26
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This is driven by continued strong demand in AI networking and start of the Google TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip production ramp which should be a strong growth driver for the Jul-Qtr and remainder of the year. We estimate the Google TPUv6 3nm (2 compute die, 8 stacks HBM3e) should drive >$15B in lifetime revenues and will be the most powerful custom XPU AI accelerator in the world. The strength in AI is led by the start of the production ramp of its TPU v6 3nm ASIC chip program with Google and we expect that to be a strong growth driver through the 2H of the year and into FY26. The TPU v6 3nm training chip is in production ramp and should contribute to AI revenues in the Jul-Qtr and 2H revenue growth driver.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Expect the start of the next-gen Tomahawk 6 (3nm) ramp to commence.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: On the networking front, we see continued strong demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and expect the start of the next-gen Tomahawk 6 (3nm) ramp to commence in the 2H of this year. In total, we expect the team to drive~$19-$20B (up 60% Y/Y) in AI revenues in FY25 on continued strong ramp of Google’s TPU AI processor, continued ramp up of its other ASIC programs ( e.g. Meta/ByteDance) and sustained demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and start of Tomahawk 6 ramp.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Continued ramp up of other ASIC programs for customers like Meta and ByteDance, contributing to FY25 AI revenues.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In total, we expect the team to drive~$19-$20B (up 60% Y/Y) in AI revenues in FY25 on continued strong ramp of Google’s TPU AI processor, continued ramp up of its other ASIC programs ( e.g. Meta/ByteDance) and sustained demand for its AI networking products (Tomahawk 5/Jericho 3 AI) and start of Tomahawk 6 ramp. • Strong AI growth driven by lead customer’s next-gen XPU program ramp and continued strong networking demand - we expect $19-$20B in AI Revenues in FY25 (up ~60% Y/Y). We expect the team to drive ~$19-$20B in AI (ASIC+networking) revenues this year (up from $12.2B last year) led by its lead XPU customer combined with incremental revenue contribution from its other AI ASIC customers (e.g. Meta/Bytedance) combined with continued strong AI networking revenues.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Gradual improvement in non-AI semi businesses on improving cyclical trends with potential revenue upside in the wireless segment.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The non-AI semi businesses should gradually improve on improving cyclical trends with potential revenue upside in the wireless segment. • Expect Jul-Qtr guide to be in-line to slightly better vs expectation on continued AI strength (custom ASICs and networking) and gradually improving trends in non-AI semi business….start of the ramp of its 3nm TPU ASIC programs... We expect the team to drive $5B+ in its AI business (vs consensus of $4.8B) combined with gradual improvements in its non-AI semi businesses.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Strong momentum in its VMware business on strong software renewals with conversion/upsell to its VCF full stack solution with higher ASPs with large corporate customers.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In the software infrastructure business, we continue to see strong momentum in its VMware business on strong software renewals (with conversion/upsell to its VCF full stack solution with higher ASPs) with its large corporate customers. • Expect in-line to slightly better Apr-Qtr results on continued strong AI demand, stabilization in non-AI semi business, and continued strong VMWare momentum. We believe that Apr quarter revenue/earnings/FCF were in-line to slightly better than ours/consensus expectations on AI strength and continued solid software renewals in its VMware business.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: AI ASIC chip tape-out for OpenAI and Softbank/ARM
catalysts.catalyst_details: Broadcom is making good progress and should be on track to tape-out new AI ASIC chips for OpenAI and Softbank/ARM.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe the team continues to make good progress at OpenAI and Softbank/ARM and should be on track to tape-out these new AI ASIC chips in 2H25.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The Meta 3nm MTIA program is on track to ramp, expected to contribute to a strong FY26 AI revenue growth profile.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: The Meta 3nm MTIA program is on track to ramp in 1H CY26 and should contribute to a strong FY26 AI revenue growth profile.

catalysts.catalyst_topic: FCF growth, dividend growth, balance sheet de-leveraging
catalysts.catalyst_details: Expect double-digit% FCF growth, supportive of continued strong dividend growth over the next several years, and continued balance sheet de-leveraging to reduce interest expense and drive earnings power.
catalysts.catalyst_dates: Next several years (for dividend growth), ongoing
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: • Strong FCF growth and capital return outlook. We expect the team to drive double-digit% FCF growth, which will be supportive of continued strong dividend growth over the next several years. In addition, we expect the team to continue to de-lever its balance sheet, which should help reduce its interest expense burden and drive earnings power.
